data.title: Coor invites to Capital Markets Day on Mar 19, 2026 in Solna, Sweden, starting at 13:00, available in person or webcast.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, the Coor Service Management Holding AB (publ) has scheduled its Capital Markets Day to be held on March 19, 2026, at the firm's headquarters in Solna, Sweden. This event offers the option to attend in person or through a webcast. The presentations are set to commence at 13:00.

data.title: In 2025, illicit stablecoin activity surged to $141B, with networks evading sanctions and laundering money. - TRM Labs
data.description: TRM Labs has revealed that illicit stablecoin usage has been primarily fueled by sanctions evasion networks, guarantee marketplaces, and extensive money laundering operations. These activities have significantly contributed to the rise in illicit stablecoin transactions, with a noticeable impact on the overall market.

data.title: Aker Solutions secures a major 5-year MMO contract with Aker BP for Norwegian Continental Shelf assets.
data.description: In Norway, on February 20, 2026, Aker Solutions secured a significant five-year contract from Aker BP for Maintenance, Modification, and Operation services (MMO). This extensive contract includes servicing all pivotal assets of Aker BP on the Norwegian Continental Shelf, encompassing the recently launched Yggdrasil.

data.title: Xerox declares quarterly dividend of $0.025/share on Common Stock and $20.00/share on Preferred Stock. Payable on specific dates.
data.description: Xerox Holdings Corporation (NASDAQ: XRX) has disclosed that its board of directors has approved quarterly dividends for shareholders. For Xerox Holdings Corporation Common Stock, a dividend of $0.025 per share has been announced, with payment scheduled for April 30, 2026, for shareholders recorded as of March 31, 2026. Additionally, for the outstanding Xerox Holdings Series A Convertible Perpetual Preferred Stock, a dividend of $20.00 per share has been declared, payable on April 1, 2026.

data.title: Renowned economist Peter Schiff warned Bitcoin could crash to $20,000, marking an 84% drop from its ATH, urging investors to sell now.
data.description: Prominent economist Peter Schiff has issued a warning about the potential crash of Bitcoin, predicting that the cryptocurrency could plummet to $20,000 or below. Schiff emphasized the urgency for investors to exit the market before it's too late. In a recent post, he highlighted the likelihood of Bitcoin dropping below $50,000 and reaching as low as $20,000, representing an 84% decrease from its all-time high. He expressed concern over the current levels of hype, leverage, institutional ownership, and market capitalization surrounding Bitcoin. Schiff took to Twitter to reiterate his advice to sell Bitcoin, especially if it surpasses the $50,000 threshold. The economist's prediction has garnered attention for its grim outlook on the future of the cryptocurrency. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: BTC selling off due to quantum fears is contradicted by ETH remaining flat, says developer Matt Carallo. Blaming quantum for BTC slump doesn't make sense.
data.description: Developers like Matt Carallo claim that individuals invested in Bitcoin are seeking to find a reason for the slow increase in the asset's value, ruling out concerns about quantum computing as the cause. Carallo's statement emphasizes that Bitcoin enthusiasts are eager to attribute the stagnant price to some external factor, with quantum fears being potentially unfounded according to his perspective.

data.title: Former Australian executive allegedly sold cyber secrets to Russia for crypto, earning around $1.26 million.
data.description: According to U.S. prosecutors, a former executive of a defense contractor received approximately $1.26 million in cryptocurrency in exchange for sharing sensitive exploit tools connected to intelligence operations of the Five Eyes alliance.
